name: codex-approval-never-safe-git-allow
doc: Codex `approval_policy=never` (AskForApproval::Never) has no interactive ask channel — Codex maps it to `permission_mode:"bypassPermissions"` on the hook payload (codex-rs/core/src/hook_runtime.rs::hook_permission_mode; NOT a config.toml read — a CLI override could contradict the on-disk file). handle-pre.ts sets neverApproval only for id==="codex" + Bash + that exact string. evaluate.ts's exemption (NEVER_SAFE subset = RALPH_SAFE + "git push", not GIT_BLOCKED, not chained, and — for push specifically — isSafePushForm) turns the usual GIT_ASK `ask` into an auto-approved `warn` — a non-blocking systemMessage, never a permissionDecision. `git push` is deliberately NOT added to RALPH_SAFE itself: RALPH_MODE has no isSafePushForm-equivalent guard, so a plain push stays ask-gated there.

steps.doc: `git commit` is in RALPH_SAFE and not destructive/chained → neverApproval auto-approves with a visible systemMessage. NO permissionDecision at all (non-blocking) — the agent-facing additionalContext carries the same auto-approve note.
`git push --force` matches GIT_BLOCKED — the neverApproval exemption explicitly requires !GIT_BLOCKED, so this stays a hard deny exactly like scenario 19/23, even under approval_policy=never.

`git push origin main` is now in NEVER_SAFE (RALPH_SAFE + "git push") — a plain push has no destructive flag (GIT_BLOCKED) and no `--delete`/`--mirror`/`--prune`/refspec-colon (isSafePushForm), so neverApproval auto-approves it exactly like `git commit` above, with a visible systemMessage. The owner's Codex autonomy no longer depends on a human/lead approving a simple push.

`git push origin --delete branch` IS in NEVER_SAFE by prefix, but isSafePushForm rejects the `--delete` flag (irreversible remote-ref deletion GIT_BLOCKED doesn't cover) — falls through to GIT_ASK, downgraded to deny on Codex exactly like a plain ask.

`git push origin :some-branch` — the legacy refspec-colon form of `--delete` (git-push(1): "prefixing all refs with a colon") — isSafePushForm's REFSPEC_COLON guard rejects it just like the `--delete` flag above, falling through to deny.

`git push origin +main` — a leading `+` on the refspec force-updates the remote ref (git-push(1): equivalent to `--force`) WITHOUT the `--force`/`-f` flag GIT_BLOCKED matches, so it slips past FORCE_FLAG. isSafePushForm's REFSPEC_FORCE guard (`\s\+\S`) rejects it, falling through to GIT_ASK → downgraded to deny on Codex. Closes the `+refspec` force-push twin of the `--force` block.

Anti-chaining regression: `git commit -m x && git push --force` must NEVER auto-approve via the NEVER_SAFE `startsWith` on the leading verb — isSingleCommand rejects the `&&`, falling through to GIT_BLOCKED's whole-string match on the trailing `git push --force` — still a hard deny.
